selectedSlice: Replace shared denied-section `unavailable` headings with explicit access-limited wording.
selectionReason: Current repo evidence showed a real protected-surface language gap, not speculative polish. The shared `renderPermissionDeniedSection()` helper still titled protected sections as `X unavailable`, which made live RBAC boundaries look like route failures across multiple roles and sections. Reusing that existing helper kept the slice small, safe, and high-visibility without touching any auth, route, or data contract.
userFacingImprovement: Denied workspace sections now explain that access is limited instead of implying the route is unavailable or broken.
privacyAndPermissionNotes: The slice changes only shared protected-surface copy in `workspace.js`.
No auth, RBAC, tenant, site, program, mentor-assignment, or student-privacy boundary changed.
No new routes, filters, buttons, or data fields were introduced.
The existing reason, owner, and next-step guidance remain protected-state only.

validation.notes: Focused workspace and audit checks passed before docs/state closeout, and the broader validation ladder passed after the closeout files were updated. git diff --check reported only CRLF normalization warnings.

deferred: Filtered Audit cross-school proof still needs a tighter locally provable scenario before another audit-focused pass.
Presentation ready-for-check-out parity proof remains smaller than a full product slice and can wait behind user-visible role-workspace work.
Student evidence-panel language cleanup remains open after this permission-boundary batch.
Site selector follow-through still needs a broader cross-surface contract.
Mentor reassignment and removal workflow still needs mutation and audit design.
Hosted section-level permission proof still needs allowed runtime and audit-event writes.
Viewer email visibility policy still needs a product decision before any UI change.
nextRecommended: Inspect filtered Audit cross-school proof next only if the current workspace can prove one exact local scenario; otherwise continue with another bounded role-workspace or student-facing language slice.
doNotRepeat: Do not regress denied protected sections back to `X unavailable` headings while the shared helper already has enough context to state that access is limited.
Do not weaken or bypass auth/RBAC checks while improving denied-section copy.
Do not turn permission-boundary cards into generic load-failure states when the route is intentionally protected.
